Operation 'translateSticker' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Current description uses vague language ('special sauce') and contains a factual error (mentions GIFs instead of stickers). Missing clarity on inputs (what phrase format is expected), outputs (single sticker vs. multiple results), and the operation's practical use case. Improved description clarifies the semantic translation mechanism, specifies input/output format, and explains when an AI agent should use this operation versus standard search.

data.current_description: The translate API draws on search, but uses the GIPHY `special sauce` to handle translating from one vocabulary to another. In this case, words and phrases to GIFs.
data.description_suggestion: Converts a text phrase or word into a relevant animated sticker using GIPHY's semantic matching algorithm. Accepts a search phrase as input and returns a single sticker that best matches the semantic meaning of the phrase. Useful for finding contextually appropriate stickers without explicit search queries.
data.reasoning: Current description uses vague language ('special sauce') and contains a factual error (mentions GIFs instead of stickers). Missing clarity on inputs (what phrase format is expected), outputs (single sticker vs. multiple results), and the operation's practical use case. Improved description clarifies the semantic translation mechanism, specifies input/output format, and explains when an AI agent should use this operation versus standard search.

data.current_description: A multiget version of the get GIF by ID endpoint.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve multiple GIFs by their IDs
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves multiple GIF objects in a single request by providing a comma-separated list of GIF IDs. Returns an array of GIF objects matching the provided IDs. This is a batch version of the single GIF retrieval endpoint, allowing efficient fetching of multiple GIFs.
data.reasoning: Original description is too vague and doesn't explain input format (comma-separated IDs), expected output (array of GIFs), or the batch nature clearly. Improved version specifies input format, return type, and use case for AI agent clarity.

message: Operation 'searchGifs' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary is adequate, but description lacks clarity on output format (paginated results), return type (GIF objects), and doesn't mention available filtering parameters. data.current_description: Search all GIPHY GIFs for a word or phrase. Punctuation will be stripped and ignored.  Use a plus or url encode for phrases. Example paul+rudd, ryan+gosling or american+psycho.
data.description_suggestion: Search the GIPHY catalog for GIFs matching a word or phrase. Punctuation is automatically stripped and ignored. Use plus signs or URL encoding for multi-word phrases (e.g., paul+rudd, ryan+gosling). Returns paginated results of matching GIF objects. Supports filtering by rating and language parameters.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate, but description lacks clarity on output format (paginated results), return type (GIF objects), and doesn't mention available filtering parameters. Enhanced version specifies pagination, return type, and parameter options for better AI agent usability.

data.current_description: The translate API draws on search, but uses the GIPHY `special sauce` to handle translating from one vocabulary to another. In this case, words and phrases to GIF
data.description_suggestion: Translates a word or phrase into a single GIF using GIPHY's semantic matching algorithm. Unlike search, this endpoint returns a single best-match GIF rather than multiple results. Accepts a search term and returns the most contextually relevant GIF object. Useful for converting natural language queries into visual representations.
data.reasoning: Original description uses vague marketing language ('special sauce') and doesn't clearly explain input requirements or output format. Improved version clarifies that it returns a single GIF (not multiple results like search), specifies input/output types, and explains the use case for AI agents.

The operation is in the stickers tag but description says GIF. data.current_description: Returns a random GIF, limited by tag. Excluding the tag parameter will return a random GIF from the GIPHY catalog.
data.description_suggestion: Returns a random sticker, optionally limited by tag. Excluding the tag parameter will return a random sticker from the GIPHY catalog. Stickers are a specialized subset of animated content optimized for messaging and communication.
data.reasoning: Description incorrectly refers to 'GIF' instead of 'sticker' (inconsistent terminology). The operation is in the stickers tag but description says GIF. Corrected version uses consistent terminology and clarifies that stickers are a specialized content type, helping AI agents understand the distinction.
